title: German Cornmeal Pudding with Resinous Gratin
description: A traditional German-inspired dessert featuring a creamy cornmeal pudding topped with a crunchy resinous gratin. Perfect for a cold evening.
instructions: 2. In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, and sugar.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, melted butter, and resinous honey.
4. Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and stir until just combined.
5. Pour the mixture into 4-6 ramekins or small baking dishes.
6. Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pudding is set and the edges are lightly golden.
7. While the pudding is baking, prepare the gratin topping.
8. In a small bowl, mix together the ingredients for the gratin topping until well combined.
9. Remove the pudding from the oven and top each ramekin with a spoonful of the gratin topping.
10. Return the ramekins to the oven and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown.
11. Remove the ramekins from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
